description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing male human figure rendered in a classical style, with a naturalistic representation of musculature and anatomy. The figure is headless and armless, with both arms broken off at the shoulders and the head missing entirely. The body is upright, with one leg slightly forward, suggesting a contrapposto stance, and the feet are firmly planted on a rectangular plinth. The surface of the sculpture is weathered, with visible discoloration and patina, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The back of the figure is supported by a vertical slab, a common feature in ancient statuary to provide structural stability. The material appears to be stone, likely marble, with a pale, off-white coloration and some areas of darker staining.
description: A weathered, headless stone sculpture of a standing male figure on a rectangular plinth, with detailed musculature and a supporting back slab.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the vertical support slab and the back of the figure, with missing head and arms clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the body, the forward step of the left leg, and the thickness of the support slab.
description_view_2: Frontal view displaying the chest, abdomen, and legs, with the absence of the head and arms most apparent.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the right arm's stump, the right leg's position, and the side of the support slab.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
